memoryArtifacts.headline: Inscription on grave of Sir Walter Griffiths, Knight
memoryArtifacts.description:
Here he the Remains of Sir WALTER GRIFFITH, Kt. who departed this life on the 9th day of August, 1481 and 
JANE his first Wife Daughter of Sir Ralph Nevill, by Mary Grandaughter to John of Gaunt, Duke of Lancaster 
In the Chapel also lie the Remains of AGNES, Second Wife of the said Sir Walter, Daughter of Sir Robert Constable of Flamborough and married secondly to Sir Gervase Clifton of Clifton, in the County of Nottingham, Kt. of the Bath She died January the 23rd, 1505.
Leaving Issue by Sir Walter Griffith aforesaid, 
WALTER his Successor made Knt. of the Bath on All-hallows Eve, 1494, at the Creation of Henry 2nd son of Henry VII. Duke of York and Agnes Married to Gervafe Clifton,
of Clifton-in-Nottinghamshire, Knt. of the Bath, Son of Sir Gervase, above mentioned.

The family takes its name from the office of constable of Chester, to which Hugh d'Avranches, 1st Earl of Chester, appointed his kinsman, Nigel, Baron of Halton, at the time of William the Conqueror. The founder of the house of Constable in Flamborough was Robert Lacy (d.1216), surnamed 'Le Constable', ancestor of Marmaduke Constable.[1]

Marmaduke Constable, born about 1480, was the second son of Sir Marmaduke Constable (c. 1456/7 – 1518), eldest son and heir of Sir Robert Constable (4 April 1423 – 23 May 1488) of Flamborough, Yorkshire, and Agnes Wentworth (d. 20 April 1496), daughter of Sir Roger Wentworth of North Elmsall, Yorkshire, by Margery le Despencer. Constable's mother was Joyce Stafford, daughter of Sir Humphrey Stafford (1400 – 7 June 1450) of Grafton, Worcestershire, slain at Sevenoaks by the rebel, Jack Cade, and Eleanor Aylesbury (born c.1406), the daughter of Sir Thomas Aylesbury (d. 9 September 1418) and his second wife, Katherine Pabenham (c.1372 – 17 June 1436). Constable had three brothers and two sisters:[2]

Sir Robert Constable (c.1478–1537), knighted at Blackheath on 17 June 1497, and executed for treason for taking part in the Pilgrimage of Grace. He married Jane Ingleby, the daughter of Sir William Ingleby of Ripley, Yorkshire, by whom he had three sons and four or five daughters.[3]
Sir William Constable of Hatfield in Holderness, knighted at Flodden.[4]
Sir John Constable (c.1491 – 1554-6) of Kinoulton, Nottinghamshire, who married, by February 1521, Jane Sothill, one of the twin daughters[5] and co-heirs of Henry Sothill, esquire, of Stoke Faston, Leicestershire, and Joan Empson, daughter of Sir Richard Empson,[6] by whom he had three daughters. He was knighted by the Earl of Hertford on 11 May 1544.[7]
Agnes Constable, who married firstly Sir Henry Ughtred, and secondly, Sir William Percy.[8]
Eleanor Constable (d.1525), who married firstly John Ingleby of Ripley, Yorkshire. She married secondly, Sir Thomas Berkeley, 5th Baron Berkeley (1472 – 22 January 1533), by whom she had two sons, Sir Thomas Berkeley, and Maurice Berkeley, esquire, and two daughters, Muriel, who married Sir Robert Throckmorton of Coughton Court, and Joan, who married Sir Nicholas Poyntz of Iron Acton, Gloucestershire.[9]
Career	Edit


Church of St Everild, Everingham, where Sir Marmaduke Constable and his wife were buried
Constable was Sheriff of Yorkshire in 1509–10, and Sheriff of Lincolnshire in 1513–14. He fought at Flodden, where his father commanded the left wing, and was knighted 9 September 1513 after the battle by Thomas Howard, then Earl of Surrey. He was in attendance at the Field of the Cloth of Gold and at King Henry VIII's meeting at Gravelines with the Emperor Charles V in 1520.[10]

Constable served in the wars in Scotland in 1522 and 1523, and played a prominent part in the capture of both Jedburgh and Ferniehurst in September 1523, where his courage earned praise from Howard, by then Duke of Norfolk. He was a member of the Council of the North from 1537 until his death.[11]

In 1529 he was elected to Parliament as a Knight of the Shire (MP) for Yorkshire, together with his first cousin, Sir John Neville. According to Bindoff, Constable was one of a number of Members, mainly politically conservative, who dined at the Queens Head to discuss parliamentary matters.[12]

In 1536 he remained loyal to the Crown when his elder brother, Sir Robert, became one of the leaders of the rebellion known as the Pilgrimage of Grace.[13] Perhaps as a reward for his loyalty, Constable was permitted to purchase Drax Priory, which had been founded by his wife's ancestors.[14]

Constable's wife, Barbara, died 4 October 1540, and was buried in the Church of St. Everild, Everingham.[15] On 2 March 1541 Constable made his will, in which he requested to be buried beside his wife. He lived for another four years, and saw military service again in Scotland in the campaign of 1544.[16] He died 14[17] September 1545, and was buried in

zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir Robert Constable VII於1423年3月27日出生在英國越可爾霍爾--斯帕爾丁-姆爾時，他的父親Sir Robert Constable VI38歲，母親Agnes Gascoigne38歲。他於大約1442年與Agnes Wentworth在英國素福爾可內特萊斯亞德結婚。他們至少是8個兒子和8個女兒的父母。他於1461年在英國越可爾被列為sheriff 。他去世於1488年5月23日在英國越可爾福藍波洛享年65歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭越可爾福藍波洛。

nameMeanings.name: Constable
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (southeastern): occupational name for the law-enforcement officer of a parish, from Middle English, Old French conestable, cunestable, from Late Latin comes stabuli ‘officer of the stable’. The title was also borne by various other officials during the Middle Ages, including the chief officer of a household or court, or a military officer, and this may in some cases be the source of the surname.
Americanized form of Dutch Constapel, an occupational name for the chief gunner aboard a ship or in the garrison of a fort.

variants.name: Costabile
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Aldo, Antonio, Ciro, Nino, Agnese, Angelo, Duilio, Enrico, Ercole, Franco, Palma.
occupational name from medieval Greek kontostablēs ‘chamberlain’, from medieval Latin comes stabuli, literally ‘count of the stable’ (see Constable ).

nameMeanings.name: Robert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the many French names of Germanic origin that were introduced into Britain by the Normans; it has since remained in continuous use. It is derived from the nearly synonymous elements hrōd ‘fame’ + berht ‘bright, famous’, and had a native Old English predecessor of similar form (Hreodbeorht), which was supplanted by the Norman name. Two dukes of Normandy in the 11th century bore the name: the father of William the Conqueror (sometimes identified with the legendary Robert the Devil), and his eldest son. It was borne also by three kings of Scotland, notably Robert the Bruce ( 1274–1329 ), who freed Scotland from English domination. The altered short form Bob is very common, but Hob and Dob, which were common in the Middle Ages and gave rise to surnames, are extinct. See also Rupert .

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir Robert Constable VII was born on 27 March 1423, in Holme-on-Spalding-Moor, Yorkshire, England, his father, Sir Robert Constable VI, was 38 and his mother, Agnes Gascoigne, was 38. He married Agnes Wentworth about 1442, in Nettlestead, Suffolk, England. They were the parents of at least 8 sons and 8 daughters. In 1461, at the age of 38, his occupation is listed as sheriff  in Yorkshire, England. He died on 23 May 1488, in Flamborough, Yorkshire, England, at the age of 65, and was buried in Flamborough, Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir Robert Constable VII est né le 27 mars 1423, à Holme-on-Spalding-Moor, Everwyck, Angleterre, son père, Sir Robert Constable VI, avait 38 ans et sa mère, Agnes Gascoigne, avait 38 ans. Il a épousé Agnes Wentworth environ 1442, à Nettlestead, Suffolk, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 8 fils et 8 filles. En 1461, à l’âge de 38 ans, sa profession de son indiquée est sheriff  à Everwyck, Angleterre. Il est décédé le 23 mai 1488, à Flamborough, Everwyck, Angleterre, à l’âge de 65 ans, et a été enterré à Flamborough, Everwyck, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir Robert Constable VII am 27. März 1423 in Holme-on-Spalding-Moor, Yorkshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Sir Robert Constable VI 38 und seine Mutter Agnes Gascoigne 38. Er hat um 1442 in Nettlestead, Suffolk, England Agnes Wentworth geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 8 Söhne und 8 Töchter. 1461, als er 38 Jahre alt war, war sein Beruf als sheriff  in Yorkshire, England aufgeführt. Er starb am 23. Mai 1488 im Alter von 65 Jahren in Flamborough, Yorkshire, England und wurde in Flamborough, Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom begraben.

Lui sposato Agnes Wentworth circa 1442, nel Nettlestead, Suffolk, England. Erano genitori di almeno 8 figli e 8 figlie. Nel 1461, all'età di 38 anni, la sua occupazione è indicata come sheriff  nel Yorkshire, England. Morì il 23 maggio 1488, nel Flamborough, Yorkshire, England, all'età di 65 anni, e fu sepolto a Flamborough, Yorkshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ko.lifeSummary: Sir Robert Constable VII(이)가 1423년 3월 27일 영국 요르크실르 홀--스팔딩-모어에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir Robert Constable VI(은)는 38세였고, 그의 어머니 Agnes Gascoigne(은)는 38세였다. 그는 대략 1442년 영국 수폴크 네틀레스테아드에서 Agnes Wentworth(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여덟 명의 아들과 여덟 명의 딸을 두었다. 1461년, 38세의 나이로 그의 직업은 영국 요르크실르에서 sheriff 이었다. 그는 1488년 5월 23일 영국 요르크실르 플램루에서 65세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 요르크실르 플램루에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Robert Constable VII nasceu em 27 de março de 1423, em Holme-on-Spalding-Moor, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir Robert Constable VI, tinha 38 anos e sua mãe, Agnes Gascoigne, tinha 38 anos. Ele casou-se com Agnes Wentworth aproximadamente 1442, em Nettlestead, Suffolk, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 8 filhos e 8 filhas. Em 1461, com 38 anos, sua profissão aparece como sheriff  em Yorkshire, Inglaterra. Ele faleceu em 23 de maio de 1488, em Flamborough, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, com 65 anos, e foi sepultado em Flamborough, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.Flamborough, East Riding of Yorkshire, E: флэмбороую, еркшир эаст ридинг, Англия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir Robert Constable VII родился 27 марта 1423 г. в местности холме-он-спалдинг-мур, еркшир, Англия, его отцу по имени Sir Robert Constable VI было 38 лет, а матери, которую звали Agnes Gascoigne, 38. Он женился на женщине по имени Agnes Wentworth около 1442 г. в местности неттлестид, суффолк, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 8 сыновей и 8 дочерей. В 1461 году, в возрасте 38 лет,  он работал по профессии sheriff  в местности еркшир, Англия. Он умер 23 мая 1488 г. в местности флэмбороую, еркшир, Англия в возрасте 65 лет и был похоронен в местности флэмбороую, еркшир, Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Sir Robert Constable VII nació el 27 de marzo de 1423, en Holme-on-Spalding-Moor, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Sir Robert Constable VI, tenía 38 años y su mamá, Agnes Gascoigne, tenía 38 años. Se casó con Agnes Wentworth aproximadamente 1442, en Nettlestead, Suffolk, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 8 hijos y 8 hijas. En 1461, a la edad de 38 años, su ocupación registrada era sheriff  en Yorkshire, Inglaterra. Murió el 23 de mayo de 1488, en Flamborough, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 65 años, y fue sepultado en Flamborough, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
